_meta.description: v2 baseline run — evolution loop ran but applied zero effective actions. All updates_run > 0 but best_state shows rim_scale=1.15 for obj_001 (minor change), others essentially at default. Treated as baseline VLM scoring reference for entire project.
_meta.important_caveat: v2 is NOT evidence that 'no action is optimal'. The loop had action-execution bugs at the time. v2 scores reflect pure baseline render quality + VLM noise, not a controlled no-action condition.

aggregate.note_on_avg: obj_002 is excluded from avg_excl_obj002 because it was chronically low-scoring due to underexposed render, not controller failure. Both averages reported for transparency.

obj_001.note: Minor rim_scale change applied but loop did not cleanly converge. Final score reflects best probe seen.

obj_002.note: Chronically underexposed render. Actions tried but no net improvement. This score is the v2 baseline for obj_002.

obj_010.note: Only object that reached >=0.80 in v2. Used as high-quality anchor throughout project.

comparison_note: v2 avg_excl_obj002=0.7477 is higher than v6/v6b averages (0.69-0.70). This is a measurement artifact: v2 used maximum-across-probes scoring, while v6+ uses mean-of-two-clean-probes (confirmed_score). An apples-to-apples retest would likely show v2 confirmed scores 0.03-0.05 lower.
